text:
The drop in price is 20.

To find the percent decrease, we use the formula:

\[
\text{Percent Decrease} = \left( \frac{\text{Drop}}{\text{Original Price}} \right) \times 100
\]

Substituting the values:

\[
\text{Percent Decrease} = \left( \frac{20}{80} \right) \times 100 = 0.25 \times 100 = 25\%
\]

So, the percent decrease is 25%.
